items.t: Christian Brothers facing bankruptcy after 183 years in Australia
items.d: Trustees seek a Creditors’ Scheme to distribute remaining assets; provision flagged for 176 ageing brothers.

items.t: Victims of Christian Brothers to share in final assets
items.d: Body responsible for restitution says it is on the brink of collapse; ~$216M to be split among survivors and creditors.

items.t: Wind-up plan risks justice for abuse survivors
items.d: “Not surprised, but appalled” — advocates warn the move echoes a Royal-Commission-documented pattern of delay.
Scheme would pay survivors more than liquidation, the group says, but less than the full value of claims.

items.t: What the proposed scheme means for survivors
items.d: Plain-English guide to the moratorium, the creditor vote and the adjudication panel.
